name: Tiramisu Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 24 ounces Cream cheese or Neufchatel cheese, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
Crust: Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Butter an 8-inch springform pan. Stir the butter and espresso powder in a small bowl until combined. Stir in crumbs until crumbs are evenly moistened. Pat evenly over bottom of prepared pan. Bake for 10 minutes. Cool on a wire rack. Keep oven on. Tightly cover the outside bottom and sides of the springform pan with heavy-duty foil.
Filling: Meanwhile, beat cream cheese and mascarpone in a large mixing bowl at medium-high speed until light and fluffy, about 2 minutes. Gradually beat in sugar, scraping down sides of the bowl with a rubber spatula, until completely smooth, about 3 minutes. Reduce speed to medium and beat in vanilla and salt. Add eggs, one at a time, beating just until blended after each addition. Pour 4 cups filling over crust in prepared pan and place in a large roasting pan. Dissolve espresso in hot water. Fold into remaining filling with brandy and grated chocolate. Spoon mixture evenly over filling in the pan; smooth top with a spatula. Place pan on oven rack. Pour enough hot water into the roasting pan to come 1 inch up the side of the springform pan. Bake for 1-1/4 hours or until center is just set. Remove cheesecake from water bath. Cool completely on a wire rack. Remove foil. Cover and refrigerate overnight. Just before serving, run a knife around edge of pan; remove sides. Sprinkle lightly with cocoa. Makes 12 servings.
